article_id: Article 10. Fundamental principles
text_snippet: The State of Rwanda commits itself to upholding the following fundamental principles and ensuring their respect: 1°. prevention and punishment of the crime of genocide, fighting against denial and rev

article_id: Article 100. Period and procedure for conducting Presidential elections
text_snippet: Elections for the President of the Republic are held at least thirty (30) days and not more than sixty (60) days before the end of the term of the incumbent President. The organic law governing electi

article_id: Article 101. Term of office of the President of the Republic
text_snippet: The President of the Republic is elected for a five (5) year term. He or she may be re-elected once.

article_id: Article 102. Oath of the President of the Republic
text_snippet: Before assuming office, the President of the Republic publicly swears the following oath before the President of the Supreme Court: “I, …………………………, do solemnly swear to Rwanda that I will: 1°. remain

article_id: Article 103. Duties incompatible with the office of the President of the Republic
text_snippet: The office of the President of the Republic is incompatible with any other elective public office, other public civilian or military positions or any other profession.

article_id: Article 104. Presidential transition
text_snippet: The incumbent President of the Republic remains in office until the President-elect assumes office. However, the incumbent President cannot, during this transitional period, exercise the following pow

article_id: Article 105. Replacing, or acting on behalf of, the President of the Republic
text_snippet: The President of the Republic ceases to hold office if he or she is definitively sentenced by the Supreme Court for treason or a serious and deliberate violation of the Constitution. The decision auth

article_id: Article 106. Powers to promulgate laws
text_snippet: The President of the Republic promulgates a law within thirty (30) days of its receipt. However, before promulgating the law, the President of the Republic may request Parliament for a second reading.

article_id: Article 107. Powers to call a referendum
text_snippet: The power to call a Referendum is vested in the President of the Republic. The President of the Republic, after consulting the Supreme Court, may call a referendum on an issue of national interest, on

article_id: Article 108. Powers of the President of the Republic in matters of war, state of siege and state of emergency
text_snippet: The President of the Republic is the Commander-in-Chief of the Rwanda Defence Force. The President of the Republic declares war. He or she signs armistice and peace agreements. The President of the Re

article_id: Article 109. Prerogative of mercy
text_snippet: The President of the Republic has the authority to exercise the prerogative of mercy in accordance with the procedures provided for by law and after consultation with the Supreme Court.

article_id: Article 11. Rwandan culture as a source of home-grown solutions
text_snippet: In order to build the nation, promote national culture and restore dignity, Rwandans, based on their values, initiate home-grown mechanisms to deal with matters that concern them. Laws may establish d

article_id: Article 110. Power to issue currency
text_snippet: The President of the Republic has the power to issue national currency in accordance with procedures determined by law.

article_id: Article 111. Power to represent the State
text_snippet: The President of the Republic represents Rwanda in its foreign relations. He or she may also designate his or her representative. The President of the Republic appoints Rwanda's Ambassadors and Specia

article_id: Article 112. Powers to enact presidential orders
text_snippet: The President of the Republic enacts presidential orders by virtue of the powers vested in him or her by this Constitution and other laws. Presidential orders on the following matters are approved by

article_id: Article 113. Benefits accorded to the President of the Republic
text_snippet: An organic law determines the benefits accorded to the President of the Republic and former Heads of State. However, when a President of the Republic was convicted of treason or of serious and deliber

article_id: Article 114. Exemption from prosecution for a former president of the Republic
text_snippet: A former President of the Republic cannot be prosecuted for treason or serious and deliberate violation of the Constitution when no legal proceedings in respect of that offence were brought against hi

article_id: Article 115. Composition of Cabinet
text_snippet: The Cabinet is composed of the Prime Minister, Ministers, State Ministers and other members who may be determined by the President of the Republic where deemed necessary.

article_id: Article 116. Appointment of Cabinet members
text_snippet: The Prime Minister is selected, appointed and dismissed by the President of the Republic. Other Cabinet members are appointed by the President of the Republic after consultation with the Prime Ministe

article_id: Article 117. Responsibilities of Cabinet
text_snippet: The Cabinet implements national policies agreed upon by the President of the Republic and the Cabinet meeting. The Cabinet is accountable to the President of the Republic and the Parliament. Modalitie

article_id: Article 118. Oath of Cabinet members
text_snippet: Before assuming office, the Prime Minister, Ministers, State Ministers and other Cabinet members publicly swear an oath before the President of the Republic.

article_id: Article 119. Responsibilities and powers of the Prime Minister
text_snippet: The Prime Minister has responsibilities and powers to: 1°. lead the functioning of Government in accordance with main guidelines given by the President of the Republic and ensure the implementation of

article_id: Article 12. Right to life
text_snippet: Everyone has the right to life. No one shall be arbitrarily deprived of life.

article_id: Article 120. Countersigning of laws and orders
text_snippet: The Prime Minister countersigns laws adopted by Parliament, decree-laws and orders, signed by the President of the Republic. Orders of the Prime Minister are countersigned by Ministers, State Minister

article_id: Article 121. Implementation of laws by Cabinet members
text_snippet: Ministers, State Ministers and other Cabinet members implement laws through orders when it is in their responsibilities.

article_id: Article 122. The Cabinet meeting
text_snippet: The Cabinet functions on the principle of collective responsibility. The Cabinet meeting deliberates on the following: 1°. draft laws and draft decree-laws; 2°. drafts of presidential orders, Prime Mi

article_id: Article 123. Incompatibilities with duties of Cabinet members as well as their benefits
text_snippet: Duties of Cabinet members are incompatible with being a member of Parliament or any other remunerated activity. Other duties incompatible with being a member of Cabinet are determined by the law on le

article_id: Article 124. Vacation of office of the Prime Minister and appointment of a new Cabinet
text_snippet: The Prime Minister's resignation or vacation of office for any reason leads to the resignation of all the other members of Cabinet. The President of the Republic receives the resignation of Cabinet wh

article_id: Article 125. Resignation of Cabinet members
text_snippet: Any Minister, State Minister or any other Cabinet member may individually tender his or her resignation in writing to the President of the Republic through the Prime Minister. The resignation becomes

article_id: Article 126. Informing Cabinet of the activities of Parliament
text_snippet: Each Chamber of Parliament informs the President of the Republic and the Prime Minister about the agenda of plenary sittings and parliamentary committees. The Prime Minister and other Cabinet members

article_id: Article 127. Vote of confidence for Government programmes
text_snippet: The Prime Minister may, upon approval by Cabinet, request the Chamber of Deputies to pass a vote of confidence either with respect to the approval of Government programmes or adoption of a draft law.

article_id: Article 128. Modalities of exercising oversight by the Chamber of Deputies over Government Activities
text_snippet: The Chamber of Deputies uses the following methods to obtain information and exercise oversight over Government activities: 1°. oral questions; 2°. written questions; 3°. hearings before Committees; 4

article_id: Article 129. Motion of no confidence against the Government, or one or more of its members
text_snippet: The Chamber of Deputies may question the performance of Cabinet, or of one or more of Cabinet members through a vote of no confidence. A motion of no confidence is only accepted after interpellation a

article_id: Article 13. Inviolability of a human being
text_snippet: A human being is sacred and inviolable. The State has an obligation to respect, protect and defend the human being.

article_id: Article 130. Resignation due to a vote of no confidence
text_snippet: A Cabinet member against whom a vote of no confidence is passed tenders his or her resignation to the President of the Republic through the Prime Minister. When the vote of no confidence is passed aga

article_id: Article 131. Modalities for exercising oversight by the Senate over Government activities
text_snippet: For purposes of obtaining information and exercising oversight over Government activities, members of the Senate may put oral or written questions to the Prime Minister to which he or she responds eit

article_id: Article 132. Dissolution of the Chamber of Deputies due to serious matters of national concern
text_snippet: Without prejudice to provisions of Articles 77 and 79 of this Constitution, the President of the Republic, after consultation with the Prime Minister, the Speaker of the Chamber of Deputies, the Presi

article_id: Article 133. Informing Parliament of Government activities
text_snippet: Once in a session of Parliament, the Prime Minister comes to inform both Chambers of Parliament in a joint sitting, about Government activities. The Prime Minister communicates Cabinet decisions to th

article_id: Article 134. Informing the Parliament of a declaration of war
text_snippet: In case the President of the Republic declares war, he or she informs Parliament, in a joint sitting within a period of seven (7) days. The Parliament adopts a vote on the matter by a simple majority

article_id: Article 135. Presidential address to Parliament
text_snippet: The President of the Republic addresses one or both Chambers of Parliament, either in person or in a message read on his or her behalf by the Prime Minister. There is no debate on such communication.

article_id: Article 136. State of Siege and state of Emergency
text_snippet: A state of siege and a state of emergency and are provided for by law and declared by the President of the Republic, following approval by Cabinet. A declaration of a state of siege or a state of emer

article_id: Article 137. Declaration of state of siege and state of emergency
text_snippet: A state of siege cannot be declared on the entire or a part of the national territory unless in the event of effective or imminent aggression by a foreign State, faces serious threat or danger to cons

article_id: Article 138. Parliament during a state of siege or state of emergency
text_snippet: During the state of siege or state of emergency, the Chamber of Deputies cannot be dissolved and both Chambers of Parliament are recalled immediately if they are in recess. If at the date of a declara

article_id: Article 139. National commissions, specialised organs, national councils and public institutions
text_snippet: The national commissions, specialised organs and national councils entrusted with the responsibility to help in resolving important issues facing the country are the following: 1°. national commission

article_id: Article 14. Right to physical and mental integrity
text_snippet: Everyone has the right to physical and mental integrity. No one shall be subjected to torture or physical abuse, or cruel, inhuman or degrading treatment. No one shall be subjected to experimentation

article_id: Article 140. National Umushyikirano Council
text_snippet: The National Umushyikirano Council brings together the President of the Republic and citizens representatives. The National Umushyikirano Council meets at least once (1) a year. It debates issues rela

article_id: Article 141. Abunzi Committee
text_snippet: The Abunzi Committee is responsible for conciliating parties in conflict with the aim of consolidating national unity and peaceful co­existence among Rwandans. The Abunzi Committee is comprised of per

article_id: Article 142. National Public Prosecution Authority
text_snippet: The National Public Prosecution Authority is responsible for investigating and prosecuting offences throughout the country. The National Public Prosecution Authority is a single institution. It is com

article_id: Article 143. Appointment of prosecutors
text_snippet: The Prosecutor General and the Deputy Prosecutor General are appointed by a presidential order after approval by the Senate. The President of the Republic nominates one candidate for each position aft

article_id: Article 144. Term of office of prosecutors in charge of prosecution services
text_snippet: The Prosecutor General and Deputy Prosecutor General are appointed for a five (5) year term, renewable once. The law governing the status of prosecutors determines the term of office for the Chief Pro

article_id: Article 145. Collaboration between the National Public Prosecution Authority and other organs
text_snippet: The National Public Prosecution Authority is under the supervision of the Minister in charge of Justice. In matters relating to prosecution of offences, the Minister in charge of justice determines th

article_id: Article 146. High Council of the National Public Prosecution Authority
text_snippet: The High Council of the National Public Prosecution Authority has the responsiblity to provide general policy guidelines and to ensure smooth functioning of the prosecution services throughout the cou

article_id: Article 147. Military Prosecution Department
text_snippet: The Military Prosecution Department is responsible for the prosecution of offences committed by persons subject to the jurisdiction of military Courts. It investigates and prosecutes offences before m

article_id: Article 148. Judicial Authority
text_snippet: The Judicial authority is vested in the Judiciary composed of ordinary Courts and specialised Courts.

article_id: Article 149. High Council of the Judiciary
text_snippet: The High Council of the Judiciary is the supreme governing organ of the Judiciary. It sets general guidelines governing the organisation of the Judiciary. A law determines the organisation, powers and

article_id: Article 15. Equality before the law
text_snippet: All persons are equal before the law. They are entitled to equal protection of the law.

article_id: Article 150. Independence of the Judiciary
text_snippet: The Judiciary is independent and exercises financial and administrative autonomy.

article_id: Article 151. Principles of the judicial system
text_snippet: The judicial system is governed by the following principles: 1°. justice is rendered in the name of the people and nobody may be a judge in his or her own cause; 2°. Court proceedings are conducted in

article_id: Article 152. Classification of Courts
text_snippet: Courts consist of ordinary and specialised Courts. Ordinary Courts are comprised of the Supreme Court, the High Court, Intermediate Courts and Primary Courts. Specialised Courts are comprised of Comme

article_id: Article 153. Appointment of Judges in charge of Courts
text_snippet: The President and Vice President of the Supreme Court, the President and Vice President of the High Court, and the President and Vice President of the Commercial High Court are appointed by a presiden

article_id: Article 154. Appointment of other Judges
text_snippet: Judges of the Supreme Court are appointed by the President of the Republic after consultation with the Cabinet and the High Council of the Judiciary. The President of the Republic submits to the Senat

article_id: Article 155. Swearing in of Judges
text_snippet: The President, Vice President, and Judges of the Supreme Court, as well as the Presidents and Vice Presidents of the High Court and Commercial High Court swear an oath of office before the President o

article_id: Article 156. Term of office of Judges in charge of Courts
text_snippet: The President and the Vice President of the Supreme Court are appointed for a five (5) year term renewable once. The President and the Vice President of the High Court, the President and the Vice Pres

article_id: Article 157. Removal of Judges
text_snippet: The President, Vice President and Judges of the Supreme Court, as well as the Presidents and Vice Presidents of the High Court and the Commercial High Court may be relieved of their duties for misbeha

article_id: Article 158. National defence and security organs
text_snippet: The State has the following defence and security organs: 1°. Rwanda Defence Force; 2°. Rwanda National Police; 3°. National Intelligence and Security Service. A law may determine other security organs

article_id: Article 159. Rwanda Defence Force
text_snippet: National defence is the responsibility of a professional national military force known as the "Rwanda Defence Force". The Chief of Defence Staff is responsible for the operations and general administr

article_id: Article 16. Protection from discrimination
text_snippet: All Rwandans are born and remain equal in rights and freedoms. Discrimination of any kind or its propaganda based on, inter alia, ethnic origin, family or ancestry, clan, skin colour or race, sex, reg

article_id: Article 160. Rwanda National Police
text_snippet: Rwanda National Police is generally responsible for ensuring security of persons and property throughout the country. A law determines the governing principles, powers, responsibilities, organisation

article_id: Article 161. National Intelligence and Security Services
text_snippet: The National Intelligence and Security Services is generally responsible for internal and external intelligence, as well as immigration and emigration matters, for the prevention of and protection aga

article_id: Article 162. State Finance law
text_snippet: Every financial year, the Chamber of Deputies considers the relevance of the State finance bill and adopts the State finance law. The State finance law determines the revenue and expenditure of the St

article_id: Article 163. Execution of budget before publication of the annual finance law
text_snippet: In case the financial year starts before the publication of the State finance law of that year, the Prime Minister authorises, by an order, a monthly expenditure on a provisional basis of an amount eq

article_id: Article 164. Imposing, modifying or removing a tax
text_snippet: Tax is imposed, modified or removed by law. No exemption or reduction of a tax can be granted unless authorised by law.

article_id: Article 165. Office of the Auditor General of State Finances
text_snippet: The Office of the Auditor General is an independent Public Institution responsible for the auditing of state finances and assets. A law determines the responsibilities, organisation and functioning of

article_id: Article 166. Report of the Auditor General of State Finances
text_snippet: The Office of the Auditor General of State Finances submits each year, to both Chambers of Parliament, prior to the commencement of the session devoted to the examination of the budget of the followin

article_id: Article 167. Negotiation and ratification of international treaties and agreements
text_snippet: The President of the Republic or his or her delegate have the power to negotiate and sign international treaties and agreements. The President of the Republic has the power to ratify international tre

article_id: Article 168. Binding force of international treaties and agreements
text_snippet: Upon publication in the Official Gazette, international treaties and agreements which have been duly ratified or approved have the force of law as national legislation in accordance with the hierarchy

article_id: Article 169. Prohibited International agreements
text_snippet: It is prohibited to make international agreements permitting foreign military bases on the national territory. It is prohibited to make international agreements permitting the transit or dumping, on n

article_id: Article 17. Right to marry and found a family
text_snippet: The right to marry and found a family is guaranteed by the law. A civil monogamous marriage between a man and a woman is the only recognised marital union. However, a monogamous marriage between a man

article_id: Article 170. International treaties and agreements conflicting with the Constitution or an organic law
text_snippet: Where an international treaty or agreement contains provisions which are conflicting with the Constitution or an organic law, the power to ratify or approve that treaty or agreement cannot be exercise

article_id: Article 171. Laws in force
text_snippet: All provisions of the Constitution of the Republic of Rwanda of 4 June 2003 and its amendments prior to this revision are repealed and replaced by this revised Constitution. However, persons elected o

article_id: Article 172. The President of the Republic
text_snippet: The President of the Republic in office at the time this revised Constitution comes into force continues to serve the term of office for which he was elected. Without prejudice to Article 101 of this

article_id: Article 173. Senators
text_snippet: Senators in office at the time this revised Constitution comes into force continue to serve the term for which they were elected or appointed to.

article_id: Article 174. The President and Vice President of the Supreme Court
text_snippet: The President and Vice President of the Supreme Court in office at the time this revised Constitution comes into force continue to serve the term to which they were appointed. CHAPTER XII. AMENDMENT O

article_id: Article 175. Procedure for amending or revising the Constitution
text_snippet: The power to initiate amendment or revision of the Constitution is vested in the President of the Republic after approval by Cabinet, or in each Chamber of Parliament through a two thirds (2/3) majori

article_id: Article 176. Commencement of laws
text_snippet: Laws and orders cannot enter into force without their prior publication in accordance with procedures determined by law. Ignorance of a duly published law is not an excuse. Unwritten customary law rem

article_id: Article 177. Commencement of this Constitution
text_snippet: This revised Constitution passed by referendum of 18/12/2015 comes into force upon promulgation by the President of the Republic and its publication in the Official Gazette of the Republic of Rwanda.

article_id: Article 18. Protection of the family
text_snippet: The family, being the natural foundation of the Rwandan society, is protected by the State. Both parents have the right and responsibility to raise their children. The State puts in place appropriate

article_id: Article 19. Child’s right to protection
text_snippet: Every child has the right to specific mechanisms of protection by his or her family, other Rwandans and the State, depending on his or her age and living conditions, as provided for by national and in

article_id: Article 2. Suffrage
text_snippet: Suffrage is universal and equal for all Rwandans. All Rwandans, both men and women, fulfilling the requirements provided for by law, have the right to vote and to be elected. Suffrage is direct or ind

article_id: Article 20. Right to education
text_snippet: Every Rwandan has the right to education. Freedom of learning and teaching is guaranteed in accordance with conditions determined by law. Primary education is compulsory and free in public schools. Co

article_id: Article 21. Right to good health
text_snippet: All Rwandans have the right to good health.

article_id: Article 22. Right to a clean environment
text_snippet: Everyone has the right to to live in a clean and healthy environment.

article_id: Article 23. Respect for privacy of a person and of family
text_snippet: The privacy of a person, his or her family, home or correspondence shall not be subjected to interference in a manner inconsistent with the law; the person's honour and dignity shall be respected. A p

article_id: Article 24. Right to liberty and security of person
text_snippet: A person's liberty and security are guaranteed by the State. No one shall be subjected to prosecution, arrest, detention or punishment unless provided for by laws in force at the time the offence was

article_id: Article 25. Right to a country and nationality
text_snippet: Every Rwandan has the right to his or her country. No Rwandan can be banished from his or her country. Every Rwandan has the right to Rwandan nationality. Dual nationality is permitted. No one can be

article_id: Article 26. Right to freedom of movement and residence
text_snippet: Every Rwandan has the right to move freely and to reside anywhere in Rwanda. Every Rwandan has the right to leave Rwanda and to return. These rights may only be restricted by law for reasons of public

article_id: Article 27. Right to participate in Government and public services
text_snippet: All Rwandans have the right to participate in the Government of the country, either directly or through their freely chosen representatives, in accordance with the law. All Rwandans have the right of

article_id: Article 28. Right to seek asylum
text_snippet: The right to seek asylum is recognised under conditions determined by law.

article_id: Article 29. Right to due process of law
text_snippet: Everyone has the right to due process of law, which includes the right: 1°. to be informed of the nature and cause of charges and the right to defence and legal representation; 2°. to be presumed inno

article_id: Article 3. Supremacy of the Constitution
text_snippet: The Constitution is the supreme law of the country. Any law, decision or act contrary to this Constitution is without effect. CHAPTER II. REPUBLIC OF RWANDA

article_id: Article 30. Right to free choice of employment
text_snippet: Everyone has the right to free choice of employment. All individuals, without any form of discrimination, have the right to equal pay for equal work.

article_id: Article 31. Right to form trade unions and employers’associations
text_snippet: The right to form trade unions for the defence and promotion of legitimate professional interests is recognised. Every worker may defend his or her rights through a trade union in accordance with the

article_id: Article 32. Right to collective bargaining
text_snippet: Trade unions and employers associations have the right to engage in collective bargaining and may enter into general or specific agreements regulating their working relations. Modalities for making th

article_id: Article 33. Right to strike
text_snippet: The right of workers to strike is permitted and is exercised within the limits provided for by the law. This right shall not be exercised in a manner that infringes upon somebody else's freedom at wor

article_id: Article 34. Right to private property
text_snippet: Everyone has the right to private property, whether individually or collectively owned. Private property, whether owned individually or collectively, is inviolable. The right to property shall not be

article_id: Article 35. Right to private ownership of land
text_snippet: Private ownership of land and other rights related to land are granted by the State. A law determines modalities of concession, transfer and use of land.

article_id: Article 36. Right to activities promoting National Culture
text_snippet: Every Rwandan has the right to activities that promote National Culture and the duty to promote it.

article_id: Article 37. Freedom of conscience and religion
text_snippet: Freedom of thought, conscience, religion, worship and public manifestation thereof is guaranteed by the State in accordance with the law. Propagation of ethnic, regional, racial discrimination or any

article_id: Article 38. Freedom of press, of expression and of access to information
text_snippet: Freedom of press, of expression and of access to information are recognised and guaranteed by the State. Freedom of expression and freedom of access to information shall not prejudice public order, go

article_id: Article 39. Right to freedom of association
text_snippet: The right to freedom of association is guaranteed and does not require prior authorisation. This right is exercised under conditions determined by law.

article_id: Article 4. The Republic
text_snippet: The Rwandan State is an independent, sovereign, democratic, social and secular Republic. The founding principle of the Republic of Rwanda is: "Government of Rwandans, by Rwandans and for Rwandans".

article_id: Article 40. Right to freedom of assembly
text_snippet: The right to freedom of peaceful and unarmed assembly is guaranteed. This right is exercised in accordance with the law. This right does not require prior authorisation, except when provided for by th

article_id: Article 41. Limitation of rights and freedoms
text_snippet: In exercising rights and freedoms, everyone is subject only to limitations provided for by the law aimed at ensuring recognition and respect of other people's rights and freedoms, as well as public mo

article_id: Article 42. Promotion of human rights
text_snippet: The promotion of human rights is a responsibility of the State. This responsibility is particularly exercised by the National Commission for Human Rights. This Commission is independent.

article_id: Article 43. Protection of rights and freedoms
text_snippet: The Judiciary is the guardian of human rights and freedoms. This duty is exercised in accordance with this Constitution and other laws. CHAPTER V. DUTIES OF THE STATE AND OF CITIZENS

article_id: Article 44. Respect for State property
text_snippet: Everyone has a duty to respect State property. State property is composed of public and private property of the State, as well as the public and private property of decentralised Government entities a

article_id: Article 45. Promotion of activities aimed at good health
text_snippet: The State has the duty to mobilise the population for activities aimed at good health and to assist them in the realisation of those activities. Every Rwandan has the duty to take part in activities a

article_id: Article 46. Maintaining of good relations with others
text_snippet: Every Rwandan has the duty to respect and consider his or her fellow beings without discrimination, and to maintain relations aimed at safeguarding, promoting and reinforcing mutual respect, solidarit

article_id: Article 47. Safeguard and promotion of national culture
text_snippet: The State has the duty to safeguard and promote national values based on cultural traditions and practices so long as they do not conflict with human rights, public order and good morals. The State al

article_id: Article 48. Participation in the development of the country
text_snippet: The State has the duty to put in place development strategies for its citizens. All Rwandans have the duty to participate in the development of the country through their dedication to work, safeguardi

article_id: Article 49. Respect of the Constitution and other laws
text_snippet: Every Rwandan has the duty to respect the Constitution and the other laws of the country. Every Rwandan has the right to defy superior orders if they constitute a serious and obvious violation of huma

article_id: Article 5. Territory of Rwanda and administrative entities
text_snippet: Rwanda's territory comprises areas covered by land, rivers, lakes and airspace located within the borders of the Republic of Rwanda. In determining Rwanda's territory, consideration is given to Rwanda

article_id: Article 50. Welfare of needy survivors of the genocide against Tutsi
text_snippet: The State, within the limits of its means and in accordance with the law, has the duty to undertake special actions aimed at the welfare of the needy survivors of the genocide against Tutsi.

article_id: Article 51. Welfare of persons with disabilities and other needy persons
text_snippet: The State has the duty to establish special measures facilitating the education of persons with disabilities. The State also has the duty, within its means, to undertake special actions aimed at the w

article_id: Article 52. Preservation of memorial sites of the genocide against Tutsi
text_snippet: The State and everyone have the duty to preserve and safeguard memorial sites of the genocide against Tutsi.

article_id: Article 53. Protection of the environment
text_snippet: Everyone has the duty to protect, safeguard and promote the environment. The State ensures the protection of the environment. A law determines modalities for protecting, conserving and promoting the e

article_id: Article 54. Recognition of political organisations
text_snippet: A multiparty system is recognised. Political organisations fulfilling the conditions required by law may be formed and operate freely. Duly registered political oganisations receive State grants. An o

article_id: Article 55. Freedom to join a political organisation
text_snippet: Every Rwandan has a right to join a political organisation of his or her choice, or not to join any. No Rwandan shall be subject to discrimination on grounds of membership in a given political organis

article_id: Article 56. Obligations of political organisations
text_snippet: Political organisations must always reflect the unity of Rwandans as well as equality and complementarity of men and women in the recruitment of members, in establishing their leadership organs, and i

article_id: Article 57. Prohibitions for political organisations
text_snippet: Political organisations are prohibited from basing themselves on race, ethnic group, tribe, lineage, region, sex, religion or any other division which may lead to discrimination.

article_id: Article 58. Holding accountable a political organisation
text_snippet: The Senate holds accountable a political organisation which has grossly violated obligations provided for in Articles 10, 56 and 57 of this Constitution. Depending on the gravity of the violation iden

article_id: Article 59. National Consultative Forum of Political Organisations
text_snippet: The National Consultative Forum of Political Organisations brings together political organisations for the purposes of political dialogue, and building consensus and national cohesion. The functioning

article_id: Article 6. Decentralisation
text_snippet: Public powers are decentralised at local administrative entities in accordance with provisions of law. A law determines the organisation and functioning of decentralised entities.

article_id: Article 60. Persons whose services are incompatible with membership in political organisations
text_snippet: Judges, prosecutors, soldiers, police officers and members of National Intelligence and Security Services are prohibited from being members of political organisations. A law may determine other person

article_id: Article 61. Branches of Government
text_snippet: Branches of Government are the following: 1°. The Legislature 2°. The Executive 3°. The Judiciary The three branches are separate and independent from each other but are all complementary. Their respo

article_id: Article 62. Power sharing
text_snippet: Power sharing is respected in State institutions in accordance with the fundamental principles set out under Article 10 of this Constitution and the provisions of other laws. The President of the Repu

article_id: Article 63. Oath of public officials
text_snippet: Officials required by this Constitution and other laws to take an oath of office, except for the President of the Republic who has a distinct oath, swear as follows: “I, ……………………, do solemnly swear to

article_id: Article 64. The Parliament
text_snippet: Legislative power is vested in a Parliament composed of two Chambers: 1°. the members of the Chamber of Deputies are known as “Deputies”; 2°. the members of the Senate are known as “Senators”. Parliam

article_id: Article 65. Guiding principles of members of Parliament
text_snippet: Every member of Parliament represents the nation as a whole and not only those who elected or nominated him or her, or the political organisation which seconded his or her candidacy during elections.

article_id: Article 66. Commencement of office for members of Parliament
text_snippet: Before assuming their duties, members of Parliament take an oath of office before the President of the Republic, or in his or her absence before the President of the Supreme Court. On commencement of

article_id: Article 67. Duties incompatible with those of a Parliamentarian
text_snippet: No one can be a member of the Chamber of Deputies and the Senate at the same time. Being a Deputy or a Senator is incompatible with being a Cabinet member. The organic laws determining the functioning

article_id: Article 68. Immunity of members of Parliament and their prosecution
text_snippet: No member of Parliament may be prosecuted, pursued, arrested, detained or judged for his or her opinion expressed or vote cast in the exercise of his or her duties. No member of Parliament suspected o

article_id: Article 69. Venue for plenary sittings of Chambers of Parliament
text_snippet: Chambers of Parliament hold their plenary sittings at designated buildings in the Capital City, except in the event of force majeure confirmed by the Supreme Court at the request of the President of t

article_id: Article 7. Capital City
text_snippet: The Capital of the Republic of Rwanda is the City of Kigali. A law determines the organisation and functioning of the Capital city. A law may relocate the Capital City elsewhere in Rwanda.

article_id: Article 70. Sittings of plenary sessions
text_snippet: For each Chamber of Parliament to duly sit, it must hold its meetings at designated buildings, upon official invitation, with an agenda, during sessions, and with a presence of at least three fifths (

article_id: Article 71. Joint sittings of the Chambers of Parliament
text_snippet: The Chambers of Parliament cannot meet in a joint sitting, except in case of matters that this Constitution or other laws require to be jointly considered, or on the occasion of national ceremonies to

article_id: Article 72. Sessions of Chambers of Parliament
text_snippet: Sittings of each Chamber of Parliament take place during ordinary and extraordinary sessions. In both Chambers of Parliament, ordinary sessions commence on the same day and have the same duration. Ord

article_id: Article 73. Functioning of each Chamber of Parliament
text_snippet: An organic law determines the functionning of each Chamber of Parliament.

article_id: Article 74. Autonomy of each Chamber of Parliament
text_snippet: Each Chamber of Parliament has its own budget and enjoys financial and administrative autonomy. Subsection 2. The Chamber of Deputies

article_id: Article 75. Composition of the Chamber of Deputies and election of its members
text_snippet: The Chamber of Deputies is composed of eighty (80) Deputies. They originate and are elected from the following categories: 1°. fifty-three (53) Deputies elected from a fixed list of names of candidate

article_id: Article 76. Term of office of Deputies
text_snippet: Deputies are elected to a five-year (5) term. They may be re-elected to additional terms.

article_id: Article 77. Vacation of office of Deputies
text_snippet: A Deputy vacates his or her seat in the following circumstances: 1°. resignation from the Chamber of Deputies; 2°. expulsion from the Chamber of Deputies; 3°. resignation from the political organisati

article_id: Article 78. Replacement of a Deputy
text_snippet: A Deputy who vacates his or her seat under the terms of Article 77 of this Constitution is replaced in accordance with the organic law governing elections.

article_id: Article 79. Dissolution of the Chamber of Deputies for election purposes
text_snippet: For elections purposes, the President of the Republic dissolves the Chamber of Deputies at least thirty (30) days and not more than sixty (60) days before the end of the parliamentary term. Elections

article_id: Article 8. National language and official languages
text_snippet: The National language is Ikinyarwanda. The official languages are Ikinyarwanda, English and French. An organic law may add or remove an official language. Official documents may be either in one, two

article_id: Article 80. Composition of the Senate
text_snippet: The Senate is composed of twenty-six (26) Senators elected or appointed as follows: 1°. twelve (12) Senators elected by specific electoral colleges in accordance with national administrative entities;

article_id: Article 81. Term of office of members of the Senate
text_snippet: Elected and appointed Senators serve a five (5) year term, renewable once. Senators who are former Heads of the State are not subject to term limits.

article_id: Article 82. Circumstances for vacation of office of a Senator
text_snippet: A Senator vacates his or her seat in the following circumstances: 1°. resignation; 2°. death; 3°. removal from office by a Court decision; or 4°. a permanent impediment to fulfil his or her responsibi

article_id: Article 83. Replacement of a Senator
text_snippet: When an elected Senator vacates his or her seat for any of the reasons specified in Article 82 of this Constitution, he or she is replaced in accordance with provisions of the organic law governing el

article_id: Article 84. Particular responsibility for the Senate
text_snippet: The Senate in particular monitors the application of fundamental principles specified in Article 10 and that of the provisions of Articles 56 and 57 of this Constitution.

article_id: Article 85. Powers of the Senate in legislative matters
text_snippet: In legislative matters, the Senate is competent to vote on the following: 1°. revision or amendment of the Constitution; 2°. organic laws; 3°. laws approving international treaties and agreements on a

article_id: Article 86. Powers of the Senate to approve the appointment of officials
text_snippet: The Senate has the powers to approve the appointment of: 1°. the President, the vice President and the Judges of the Supreme Court, the President and Vice President of the High Court and of the Commer

article_id: Article 87. Transmission of draft laws to the Senate
text_snippet: The Speaker of the Chamber of Deputies transmits to the President of the Senate draft laws adopted by the Chamber of Deputies, relating to matters specified in Article 85 of this Constitution. Subsect

article_id: Article 88. Right to initiate and amend laws
text_snippet: Initiation and amendment of laws is the right of every Deputy or the Government acting through Cabinet. However, the Senate initiates the draft organic law determining the functionning of the Senate.

article_id: Article 89. Draft of a law likely to affect the national budget
text_snippet: If a draft or an amendment of a law has the potential to reduce Government revenue or increase State expenditure, the initiator must indicate how the State will raise revenues or make savings equivale

article_id: Article 9. National Symbols of Rwanda
text_snippet: The national symbols of Rwanda are the National flag, the Motto of the Republic, the Seal of the Republic and the National Anthem. The flag comprises the following colours: from bottom to top a green

article_id: Article 90. Examination of draft laws by Committees
text_snippet: Draft laws determined by the plenary sitting to have relevance are transmitted to the relevant parliamentary committee of the Chamber of Parliament for examination prior to their consideration and ado

article_id: Article 91. Procedures for adoption of law
text_snippet: Ordinary laws are passed by an absolute majority vote of Deputies or Senators present. Organic laws are passed by a three fifths (3/5) majority vote of Deputies or Senators present entitled to vote. T

article_id: Article 92. Initiation and adoption of decree-laws
text_snippet: If it is absolutely impossible for the Parliament to sit, the President of the Republic may during that time promulgate decree-laws approved by Cabinet. These decree-laws have the same force as ordina

article_id: Article 93. Urgent consideration of a draft law or any other matter
text_snippet: Urgent consideration of a draft law or any other matter may be requested by either a member of Parliament or by the Government by petitioning the relevant chamber of Parliament. When the petition is s

article_id: Article 94. Joint committee of Parliament
text_snippet: Draft laws subject to examination by the Senate are transmitted to the Senate after adoption by the Chamber of Deputies. If the draft law is not approved by the Senate or the amendments proposed by th

article_id: Article 95. Hierarchy of laws
text_snippet: The hierarchy of laws is as follows: 1°. Constitution; 2°. organic law; 3°. international treaties and agreements ratified by Rwanda; 4°. ordinary law; 5°. orders. A law cannot contradict another law

article_id: Article 96. Authentic interpretation of laws
text_snippet: Authentic interpretation of laws is done by the Supreme Court. Authentic interpretation of laws may be requested by Cabinet or the Bar Association. Any interested person may request for an authentic i

article_id: Article 97. Exercise of Executive Power
text_snippet: Executive Power is vested in the President of the Republic and in Cabinet. Subsection One. The President of the Republic

article_id: Article 98. Responsibilities of the President of the Republic
text_snippet: The President of the Republic is the Head of State. The President of the Republic is the defender of the Constitution and the guarantor of national unity. The President of the Republic ensures the con

article_id: Article 99. Requirements of a candidate for the office of President of the Republic
text_snippet: A candidate for the office of the President of the Republic must: 1°. be of Rwandan nationality by origin; 2°. not hold any other nationality; 3°. be irreproachable in his or her conduct and social re

article_id: Preamble
text_snippet: Preamble We, the People of Rwanda, HONOURING our valiant ancestors who sacrificed themselves to found Rwanda and the heroes who struggled for security, justice, freedom, and the restoration of our nat
